description_long: This is a single-bladed axe with a robust, dark metal head and a gently curved wooden handle. The handle features a textured grip section, likely wrapped or treated for enhanced hold, and transitions from a reddish-brown wood to a darker, almost black grip area. The axe head is broad with a pronounced cutting edge and a squared poll, suitable for both chopping and splitting tasks. The end of the handle is capped with a lighter, possibly metallic or reinforced section, providing durability and balance. The overall design suggests a tool built for heavy-duty use, with ergonomic considerations for comfort and control. No visible branding or decorative engravings are present, giving it a utilitarian appearance.
description: A robust, single-bladed axe with a curved wooden handle, dark metal head, and a textured grip for secure handling. The design is utilitarian, with a reinforced handle end and no visible branding.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the curved handle, the broad axe blade, and the reinforced end cap.
description_view_1: End-on view of the handle, highlighting the circular cross-section and the light-colored end cap.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the handle's curvature, the blade's profile, and the grip texture.
description_view_3: Blade-on view showing the cutting edge, the thickness of the blade, and the alignment of the handle.
attribute_handle_type: Curved wooden handle with a textured, dark grip section and a rounded, reinforced end cap; oval cross-section for ergonomic grip.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad blade with a straight cutting edge, squared poll, and no visible beard; functional and robust geometry.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with the blade fitted onto the handle and secured, possibly with a metal wedge or pin.
